etymology.description: semantic 旨(旨) (zhǐ, "tasty") + phonetic 尚(尚) (shàng) -> taste
etymology.interpretation: The character 嘗 represents the act of tasting, with 尚(尚) providing the sound and 旨(旨) contributing the meaning of 'tasty'. The semantic component 旨(旨) evokes the idea of a delicious flavor, leading naturally to the concept of tasting or trying food.
etymology.simplification: 尝(嘗) reduced the character by removing the semantic element 旨(旨), leaving only the phonetic 尚(尚).

examples.chinese: 我想尝一下这道菜(我想嘗一下這道菜)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒ xiǎng cháng yīxià zhè dào cài.
examples.english: I want to taste this dish.

examples.chinese: 她总是尝试新事物(她總是嘗試新事物)。
examples.pinyin: Tā zǒng shì chángshì xīn shìwù.

examples.chinese: 我们在集市上尝了很多美食(我們在集市上嘗了很多美食)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒmen zài jíshì shàng cháng le hěn duō měishí.
examples.english: We tasted a lot of delicacies at the market.
